publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-17 23:59:48 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, news clippings, scripts, programs, playbills, lecture notes, theater notes, photographs, recordings and other memorabilia.
did.abstract: Correspondence, news clippings, scripts, programs, playbills, lecture notes, theater notes, photographs, recordings and other memorabilia relating to various aspects of the field of Speech and Drama.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Speech and Drama, Cornell University.
Organized the Cornell Dramatic Club and was its Director from 1912-1947. Wrote original plays and a monograph on play production.

emph.ref: Series V. Photographs and Glass Slides

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Includes collection #14-24-1143. See also Walter Stainton papers. #14-24-573.

list.item: Kingsley, Sidney. Men in White. Signed by the author.
Hatch, Emily Gilchriest, 1897-. On the road to market. Trivandrum : Keston House, 1938.
The Public Speaking Review. Public Speaking Conference of New England and the North Atlantic States.
World premieres : monthly bulletin of the International Theatre Institute. International Theatre Institute. v.1, no.3-6 (1949-1950).

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Alexander M. Drummond papers, #14-24-435. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, news clippings, scripts, programs, playbills, lecture notes, theater notes, photographs, recordings and other memorabilia relating to various aspects of the field of Speech and Drama.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes charcoal drawing of Professor Drummond by Christian Midjo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Eugene O'Neill, Oscar Hammerstein II, George Bernard Shaw and Hendrik Van Loon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, etc. with the Speech Association of America relating to graduate research, 1947.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text of speeches given by Dean Paul M. O'Leary, President Deane W. Malott, Professor Herbert A. Wichelns and Professor Drummond at a testimonial dinner for Drummond, May 14, 1952.

controlaccess.subject: Theatrical publishing.
Theater reviews.
College theater -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Drama -- Study and teaching.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Department of Speech and Drama
